skill.name: global-tech-stack
skill.description: Guide AI coding assistants to follow a Laravel, PHP, React, PostgreSQL, Pest, Tailwind CSS, and GitHub Actions technology stack.
skill.summary: Apply established Laravel, React, database, testing, and tooling choices across project work.
skill.author: DevanB

security_audit.summary: Static path traversal detections at SKILL.md line 29 are not executable code, but they reference a file outside the skill package. Weak cryptography detections at lines 3 and 8 are false positives from prose text, with no cryptographic operation found.

medium_findings.title: Out-of-Package File Reference
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md line 29 links to ../../../agent-os/standards/global/tech-stack.md. This is a Markdown reference, not code execution, but it asks the assistant to rely on content outside the audited skill package.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced path is directly visible and contains three parent-directory segments. The risk is contextual because it expands required review scope, but no malicious payload is present in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections at SKILL.md lines 3 and 8 occur in descriptive prose. No evidence found of DES, MD5, SHA-1, key handling, hashing, encryption, or crypto API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both flagged locations are plain English skill descriptions. There are no scripts, imports, command calls, or cryptographic operations in the audited file.

content.user_title: Align Projects With a Laravel Tech Stack
content.value_statement: Teams often lose consistency when AI assistants choose tools without project context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward the approved Laravel-centered stack.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when project decisions should follow the approved technology stack.
Guides Laravel, PHP, React, PostgreSQL, Pest, and Tailwind CSS choices.
Promotes use of named Laravel tools, including Pint, Herd, Telescope, DebugBar, Wayfinder, and Fortify.
Keeps third-party service guidance aligned with Resend, Sentry, Redis, and GitHub Actions.
Encourages consistent technical documentation for dependencies and architecture decisions.
limitations: It depends on a referenced standards file outside the skill package.
It does not include runnable scripts, validators, or automated configuration checks.
It does not provide detailed implementation steps for each listed framework.
It is tailored to one Laravel and React application stack.

use_cases.title: Choose Feature Architecture
use_cases.description: Use the skill to keep new feature designs aligned with Laravel, React, PostgreSQL, and existing project tools.

use_cases.title: Review Package Choices
use_cases.description: Use the skill to evaluate whether proposed libraries match the approved stack and project conventions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Project Setup
use_cases.description: Use the skill to guide local development, testing, and CI choices for a Laravel application.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Simple Choice
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a framework or package decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the global-tech-stack skill, check whether this package choice fits our Laravel and React stack: [describe choice].

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning for a new application feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-tech-stack skill to plan this feature with Laravel, React, PostgreSQL, Pest, and Tailwind CSS: [feature summary].

prompt_templates.title: Review Technical Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the global-tech-stack skill to review this implementation plan for stack consistency, missing tools, and risky deviations: [plan].

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Cross-Service Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced architecture review across services and operations.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the global-tech-stack skill to audit this design for Laravel tooling, services, caching, testing, CI, and documentation alignment: [design].

output_examples.input: Review a proposed email integration for the project.
output_examples.output: The recommendation should prefer Resend because the skill lists it as the project email service. It should also note testing and error handling expectations.

output_examples.input: Plan database work for a new reporting page.
output_examples.output: The response should favor PostgreSQL and Eloquent patterns, include Pest coverage, and avoid suggesting unrelated database tools without justification.

output_examples.input: Choose a local development setup for a new teammate.
output_examples.output: The response should recommend Laravel Herd when relevant and keep setup guidance consistent with the listed Laravel tools.

best_practices: Use this skill before choosing new packages or services.
Pair the skill with the current project standards file when available.
Ask for explicit tradeoffs when a request may deviate from the approved stack.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a general Laravel tutorial.
Do not treat it as a substitute for security review.
Do not follow the external referenced file unless it has been reviewed.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding assistants keep architecture, tooling, testing, and service choices aligned with a defined Laravel and React stack.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No. The audited SKILL.md file contains guidance text and a Markdown link, not scripts or executable commands.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill references a file outside its package. That external content should be reviewed before publication or use.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it choose new libraries?
faq.answer: It can guide library evaluation against the approved stack, but final choices still need human review.

faq.question: Is this skill project-specific?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed for a Laravel, PHP, React, PostgreSQL, Pest, Tailwind CSS, and GitHub Actions environment.
